chunk_summary: This section outlines CMAM program performance indicators and Sphere benchmarks for managing severe acute malnutrition, emphasizing inpatient and outpatient quality metrics, operational indicators, and system-level monitoring for improved outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand Sphere benchmarks for outpatient therapeutic feeding programs and their application.
Identify key inpatient and outpatient quality signals for SAM management.
Learn operational indicators predicting individual failure in CMAM programs.
Explore system-level monitoring strategies for non-response cases in SAM management.
key_facts: Sphere standards for outpatient programs: recovered >75%, died <10%, defaulted <15%.
Inpatient death thresholds are expected to be lower than outpatient benchmarks due to timely stabilization and minimized iatrogenic harm.
High-yield inpatient quality signals include deaths within the first 48–72 hours, inappropriate IV fluid use, and adherence to phased feeding protocols.
Non-response indicators: appetite not regained by day 4, oedema persisting to day 10, weight gain <5 g/kg/day after catch-up initiation.
Operational indicators include median length of stay and proportion referred to inpatient care for complications.
reference_quote: Sphere standards for outpatient therapeutic feeding programs: recovered >75%, died <10%, and defaulted <15% among discharges.
